---
title: "Context Group Analysis"
format: html
editor: visual
---


## Section 1: Load Data

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Step 1: Load the Excel file into R
context_df <- read_xlsx(file.choose())
```

## Section 2: Drop Qualtrics Metadata Columns

```{r}
library(dplyr)

context_df <- context_df %>%
  select(-c(
    StartDate, EndDate, Status, IPAddress, Progress, Finished, RecordedDate, ResponseId,
    RecipientLastName, RecipientFirstName, RecipientEmail, ExternalReference, LocationLatitude, LocationLongitude,
    DistributionChannel, UserLanguage
  ))

# Display the updated dataframe
context_df <- context_df %>%
  select(
-contains("15-second pause"),
    -contains("20-second delay"),
    - contains("pause_"),
    - contains("Q3_"))



```

## Section 3: Headphone Check Filtering

failed headphone checks 6.0 (correct answer) for column Q2...22 5.0 (correct answer) for column Q2...23 6.0 (correct answer) for column Q2...24

Anyone who got a wrong answer will be removed! Then, I will count the number of IDs (people still left)

```{r}
# Check individual conditions
context_clean <- context_df[-1, ]
context_df <- context_df[-1, ]


# Convert columns to numeric
context_clean <- context_clean %>%
  mutate(across(c(`Q2...22`, `Q2...23`, `Q2...24`), as.numeric))

context_df <- context_df %>%
  mutate(across(c(`Q2...22`, `Q2...23`, `Q2...24`), as.numeric))

# Handle missing values
context_clean <- context_clean %>%
  filter(!is.na(`Q2...22`) & !is.na(`Q2...23`) & !is.na(`Q2...24`)) %>%
  filter(`Q2...22` == 6.0 & 
         `Q2...23` == 5.0 & 
         `Q2...24` == 6.0)


# Handle missing values
context_df <- context_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(`Q2...22`) & !is.na(`Q2...23`) & !is.na(`Q2...24`)) %>%
  filter(`Q2...22` == 6.0 & 
         `Q2...23` == 5.0 & 
         `Q2...24` == 6.0)


```

## Section 4: Extract Ease-of-Context Ratings

```{r}
# Step 2: Extract the columns Aud01_1 to Aud08_1
aud_columns <- context_clean %>% 
  select(starts_with("Aud") & ends_with("_1"))

# Ensure all values are numeric and remove trailing .0
aud_columns[] <- lapply(aud_columns, function(x) {
  # Convert to numeric if possible
  x <- as.numeric(as.character(x))
  # Return the numeric values
  return(x)
})

# Check for any NA values (which would indicate non-numeric data)
sapply(aud_columns, function(x) sum(is.na(x)))
```

## Section 5: Pivot to Long Format and Label Stimuli

```{r}
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Convert wide data to long format
aud_long <- aud_columns %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to = "Audio", values_to = "Rating")

# Make sure Rating is numeric (if not already)
aud_long$Rating <- as.numeric(aud_long$Rating)

```

```{r}
# Define your label mapping
label_mapping <- c(
  "Aud01_1" = "5SNF1",
  "Aud02_1" = "5SNS2",
  "Aud05_1" = "5NNF5",
  "Aud06_1" = "5NNS6",
  "Aud09_1" = "3SNF9",
  "Aud10_1" = "3SNS10",
  "Aud11_1" = "3S6F11",
  "Aud12_1" = "3S6S12"
)

# Update the Audio column
aud_long$Audio <- label_mapping[aud_long$Audio]

# Check the unique values to confirm
unique(aud_long$Audio)

```


## Section 6: Add Acoustic Feature Columns to aud_long

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)

library(stringr)
aud_long <- aud_long %>%
  mutate(Aud_num = as.integer(str_extract(Audio, "\\d+$")))

# Create a new column to group Aud 1, 2, 5, 6 as '500 ms' and the rest as '3000 ms'
aud_long$Duration <- ifelse(aud_long$Aud_num %in% c(1, 2, 5, 6), "500 ms", "3000 ms")

# Create a new column to group Aud 1 & 2 vs 5 & 6 for different timbres
aud_long$Timbre <- ifelse(aud_long$Aud_num %in% c(1, 2, 9, 10, 11, 12), "sine", 
                                  ifelse(aud_long$Aud_num %in% c(5, 6), "noise", 
                                         "Other"))

# Create a new column to group Aud 9 & 10 as 'none' and Aud 11 & 12 as '6 Hz'
aud_long$warble <- ifelse(aud_long$Aud_num %in% c(1, 2, 5, 6, 9, 10), "none", 
                                ifelse(aud_long$Aud_num %in% c(11, 12), "6 Hz", 
                                       "Other"))
# Create a new column to group Aud 1, 5, 9, 11 as 'fast' and the rest as 'slow'
aud_long$AttackTimes <- ifelse(aud_long$Aud_num %in% c(1, 5, 9, 11), "fast", "slow")



```

## Section 7: Create context_long for LMM

Reshapes context_clean to long format for the LMM

```{r}
context_ANOVA <- context_clean %>%
  select(ID, matches("^Aud.*_1$"))

library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)

# Reshape to long format
context_long <- context_ANOVA %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Aud"),  # Select all audio columns
    names_to = "Audio",         # New column for audio names (e.g., "Aud01_1")
    values_to = "Rating"        # New column for ratings/scores
  )


library(stringr)
context_long <- context_long %>%
  mutate(Audio = as.integer(str_extract(Audio, "\\d+")))


# Create a new column to group Aud 1 & 2 vs 5 & 6 for different durations
context_long$Duration <- ifelse(context_long$Audio %in% c(1, 2, 5, 6), "500 ms", 
                                  ifelse(context_long$Audio %in% c(9, 10, 11, 12), "3000 ms", 
                                         "Other"))

# Create a new column to group Aud 1 & 2 vs 5 & 6 for different timbres
context_long$Timbre <- ifelse(context_long$Audio %in% c(1, 2, 9, 10, 11, 12), "sine", 
                                  ifelse(context_long$Audio %in% c(5, 6), "noise", 
                                         "Other"))

# Create a new column to group Aud 9 & 10 as 'none' and Aud 11 & 12 as '6 Hz'
context_long$warble <- ifelse(context_long$Audio %in% c(1, 2, 5, 6, 9, 10), "none", 
                                ifelse(context_long$Audio %in% c(11, 12), "6 Hz", 
                                       "Other"))
# Create a new column to group Aud 1, 5, 9, 11 as 'fast' and the rest as 'slow'
context_long$AttackTimes <- ifelse(context_long$Audio %in% c(1, 5, 9, 11), "fast", "slow")


```

## Section 8: Descriptive Statistics

```{r}
library(dplyr)

cat("CONTEXT RATINGS: DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS\n")

# Overall statistics
cat("OVERALL\n")
overall_stats <- aud_long %>%
 summarise(
   M = mean(Rating, na.rm = TRUE),
   SD = sd(Rating, na.rm = TRUE),
   Min = min(Rating, na.rm = TRUE),
   Max = max(Rating, na.rm = TRUE),
   N = n()
 )
print(overall_stats)
cat("\n")

# By individual stimulus with IQR
cat("BY INDIVIDUAL STIMULUS\n")
by_stimulus <- aud_long %>%
 group_by(Audio) %>%
 summarise(
   M = mean(Rating, na.rm = TRUE),
   SD = sd(Rating, na.rm = TRUE),
   Median = median(Rating, na.rm = TRUE),
   IQR = IQR(Rating, na.rm = TRUE),
   Min = min(Rating, na.rm = TRUE),
   Max = max(Rating, na.rm = TRUE),
   N = n(),
   .groups = 'drop'
 )
print(by_stimulus)
cat("\n")

# By Duration
cat("BY DURATION\n")
by_duration <- aud_long %>%
 group_by(Duration) %>%
 summarise(
   M = mean(Rating, na.rm = TRUE),
   SD = sd(Rating, na.rm = TRUE),
   Median_IQR = sprintf("%.1f (%.1f)", median(Rating, na.rm = TRUE), IQR(Rating, na.rm = TRUE)),
   range = sprintf("%.2fâ€“%.2f", min(Rating, na.rm = TRUE), max(Rating, na.rm = TRUE)),
   n_stimuli = n_distinct(Audio),
   N_responses = n(),
   .groups = 'drop'
 )
print(by_duration)
cat("\n")

# By Timbre
cat("BY TIMBRE\n")
by_timbre <- aud_long %>%
 group_by(Timbre) %>%
 summarise(
   M = mean(Rating, na.rm = TRUE),
   SD = sd(Rating, na.rm = TRUE),
   Median_IQR = sprintf("%.1f (%.1f)", median(Rating, na.rm = TRUE), IQR(Rating, na.rm = TRUE)),
   range = sprintf("%.2fâ€“%.2f", min(Rating, na.rm = TRUE), max(Rating, na.rm = TRUE)),
   n_stimuli = n_distinct(Audio),
   N_responses = n(),
   .groups = 'drop'
 )
print(by_timbre)
cat("\n")

# By Warble
cat("BY WARBLE\n")
by_warble <- aud_long %>%
 group_by(warble) %>%
 summarise(
   M = mean(Rating, na.rm = TRUE),
   SD = sd(Rating, na.rm = TRUE),
   Median_IQR = sprintf("%.1f (%.1f)", median(Rating, na.rm = TRUE), IQR(Rating, na.rm = TRUE)),
   range = sprintf("%.2fâ€“%.2f", min(Rating, na.rm = TRUE), max(Rating, na.rm = TRUE)),
   n_stimuli = n_distinct(Audio),
   N_responses = n(),
   .groups = 'drop'
 )
print(by_warble)
cat("\n")

# By Attack Time
cat("BY ATTACK TIME\n")
by_attack <- aud_long %>%
 group_by(AttackTimes) %>%
 summarise(
   M = mean(Rating, na.rm = TRUE),
   SD = sd(Rating, na.rm = TRUE),
   Median_IQR = sprintf("%.1f (%.1f)", median(Rating, na.rm = TRUE), IQR(Rating, na.rm = TRUE)),
   range = sprintf("%.2fâ€“%.2f", min(Rating, na.rm = TRUE), max(Rating, na.rm = TRUE)),
   n_stimuli = n_distinct(Audio),
   N_responses = n(),
   .groups = 'drop'
 )
print(by_attack)
```

Overall M and SD 

```{r}
# Overall M and SD (across all trials)
context_overall <- aud_long %>%
  summarise(
    M = mean(Rating, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD = sd(Rating, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
print(context_overall)

# Range of stimulus means
context_by_stimulus <- aud_long %>%
  group_by(Audio) %>%
  summarise(M = mean(Rating, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

cat("Context range:", round(min(context_by_stimulus$M), 2), "to", round(max(context_by_stimulus$M), 2))
print(context_by_stimulus)
```

## Section 9: Ease-of-Context LMM  

LMM: Rating ~ Duration + Timbre + warble + AttackTimes + (1|ID)

```{r}
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(car)
library(MuMIn)
library(broom.mixed)

# Ensure factors are set correctly
context_long <- context_long %>%
 mutate(
   Duration = as.factor(Duration),
   Timbre = as.factor(Timbre),
   warble = as.factor(warble),
   AttackTimes = as.factor(AttackTimes),
   ID = as.factor(ID),
   Rating = as.numeric(Rating)
 )

# Set reference levels
context_long$Timbre <- relevel(context_long$Timbre, ref = "sine")
context_long$Duration <- relevel(context_long$Duration, ref = "3000 ms")
context_long$warble <- relevel(context_long$warble, ref = "none")
context_long$AttackTimes <- relevel(context_long$AttackTimes, ref = "slow")

# Fit the main effects model
context_lmm <- lmer(
 Rating ~ Duration + Timbre + warble + AttackTimes + (1 | ID),
 data = context_long,
 REML = TRUE
)

cat("CONTEXT GROUP: LINEAR MIXED-EFFECTS MODEL RESULTS\n")

# Model summary
cat("MODEL SUMMARY\n")
print(summary(context_lmm))
cat("\n")

# Type III ANOVA table
cat("TYPE III ANOVA TABLE\n")
anova_results <- car::Anova(context_lmm, type = 3)
print(anova_results)
cat("\n")

# Fixed effects with 95% CIs
cat("FIXED EFFECTS WITH 95% CONFIDENCE INTERVALS\n")
tidy_fixed <- tidy(context_lmm, effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE)
print(tidy_fixed)
cat("\n")

# Model fit statistics
cat("MODEL FIT STATISTICS\n")
r2_values <- MuMIn::r.squaredGLMM(context_lmm)
cat(sprintf("Marginal RÂ˛ (fixed effects only): %.4f\n", r2_values[1]))
cat(sprintf("Conditional RÂ˛ (fixed + random): %.4f\n", r2_values[2]))
cat(sprintf("AIC: %.2f\n", AIC(context_lmm)))
cat(sprintf("BIC: %.2f\n", BIC(context_lmm)))
cat("\n")

# Practical interpretation
cat("PRACTICAL INTERPRETATION\n")
coefs <- fixef(context_lmm)
cat(sprintf("Timbre effect: Noise stimuli yield context ratings %.3f points higher than sine (on 6-point scale)\n",
           coefs["Timbrenoise"]))
cat(sprintf("Duration effect: 500ms stimuli yield context ratings %.3f points different from 3000ms\n",
           coefs["Duration500 ms"]))
cat(sprintf("Warble effect: No warble yields context ratings %.3f points different from 6Hz warble\n",
           -coefs["warble6 Hz"]))  # Negative because we want none vs 6Hz
```

```{r}
cat("Context Ease-of-Context LMM\n")

tidy_ctx <- tidy(context_lmm, effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE)
coefs_ctx <- fixef(context_lmm)
r2_ctx <- MuMIn::r.squaredGLMM(context_lmm)

# Print each predictor in Sage format
for (i in 2:nrow(tidy_ctx)) {
  row <- tidy_ctx[i, ]
  cat(sprintf("%s: b = %.2f, SE = %.2f, 95%% CI [%.2f, %.2f], t(%.1f) = %.2f, p = %s\n",
      row$term,
      row$estimate,
      row$std.error,
      row$conf.low,
      row$conf.high,
      row$df,
      row$statistic,
      ifelse(row$p.value < .001, "< .001", sprintf("%.3f", row$p.value))
  ))
}

cat(sprintf("\nRÂ˛m = %.4f, RÂ˛c = %.4f\n", r2_ctx[1], r2_ctx[2]))
```
